name: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose Ophthalmic Solution USP
description: Sterile viscoelastic ophthalmic solution supplied in a prefilled syringe presentation for anterior segment surgical support.
usageSummary: Used by ophthalmic surgeons during cataract and intraocular procedures where chamber maintenance, tissue protection, and controlled sterile handling are important.

variants.composition: Each ml contains: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose USP 20 mg, in sterile balanced salt vehicle q.s.
variants.usage: Commonly reviewed for cataract OT supply where surgeons need a sterile, ready-to-use viscoelastic presentation.

name: Sodium Hyaluronate Ophthalmic Solution BP
description: Ophthalmic viscosurgical device supplied as a sterile, ready-to-use prefilled syringe for anterior segment procedures.
usageSummary: Used in ophthalmic surgery settings to support intraocular tissue protection and space maintenance during delicate anterior segment procedures.

variants.composition: Each ml contains: Sodium Hyaluronate BP 10 mg, in sterile phosphate-buffered vehicle.
variants.usage: Suited for ophthalmic OT procurement where a ready-to-use sterile viscosurgical syringe is preferred.

name: Moxifloxacin Ophthalmic Solution
description: Fourth-generation fluoroquinolone ophthalmic antibiotic for bacterial conjunctivitis and peri-operative infection-control protocols.
usageSummary: Used in ophthalmology OPD, surgical support, and hospital pharmacy supply where topical antibiotic coverage is required as prescribed.

variants.composition: Each ml contains: Moxifloxacin HCl equivalent to Moxifloxacin 5 mg.
variants.usage: Typically stocked for ophthalmology departments and prescription-led outpatient eye care.

name: Enoxaparin Sodium Injection IP
description: Low molecular weight heparin (LMWH) supplied in prefilled syringe presentations for hospital anticoagulation protocols.
usageSummary: Used in critical care, cardiology, and post-operative hospital settings where clinicians require protocol-led anticoagulation support.

variants.composition: Each PFS contains: Enoxaparin Sodium IP 40 mg in 0.4 ml aqueous vehicle.
variants.usage: Often reviewed for prophylaxis-oriented hospital protocols and post-surgical ward requirements.

variants.composition: Each PFS contains: Enoxaparin Sodium IP 60 mg in 0.6 ml aqueous vehicle.
variants.usage: Often reviewed for higher-strength cardiology, ICU, and acute-care anticoagulation protocols.

name: Heparin Sodium Injection IP
description: Heparin Sodium PFS for anticoagulation support in critical care, dialysis, and institutional hospital settings.
usageSummary: Used by hospital teams for clinician-directed anticoagulation support and catheter-related protocols in acute care environments.

variants.composition: Each ml contains: Heparin Sodium IP 5000 IU in aqueous vehicle.
variants.usage: Commonly evaluated for institutional supply to critical care and dialysis departments.

name: Sodium Hyaluronate Intra-articular Injection
description: Sterile intra-articular hyaluronic acid PFS for orthopaedic visco-supplementation and knee osteoarthritis support.
usageSummary: Used in orthopaedic clinics and procedure rooms where clinicians administer intra-articular visco-supplementation under appropriate medical supervision.

variants.composition: Each PFS contains: Sodium Hyaluronate sterile solution as per label claim.
variants.usage: Typically reviewed for orthopaedic OPD and day-care procedure supply.

name: Diclofenac Sodium Injection IP
description: NSAID injection in a ready-to-administer PFS format for post-operative and musculoskeletal pain-management protocols.
usageSummary: Used in orthopaedic, surgical, and emergency settings where clinicians need injectable analgesic support as per approved protocols.

variants.composition: Each ml contains: Diclofenac Sodium IP 75 mg in injectable vehicle.
variants.usage: Commonly considered for hospital pain-management inventory and emergency department availability.

name: Iron Sucrose Injection USP
description: Sterile parenteral iron preparation for iron deficiency anaemia support in obstetric, gynaecology, and nephrology patients.
usageSummary: Used in obstetric, gynaecology, nephrology, and infusion settings where parenteral iron therapy is prescribed and monitored.

variants.composition: Each 5 ml contains: Iron Sucrose USP equivalent to 100 mg of elemental iron.
variants.usage: Typically reviewed for hospital infusion areas, obstetric care, and anaemia-management programmes.

name: Tranexamic Acid Injection IP
description: Anti-fibrinolytic injection for control and prophylaxis of heavy menstrual bleeding, post-partum haemorrhage, and surgical bleeding.
usageSummary: Used in emergency, obstetric, gynaecology, and surgical settings where clinician-led bleeding-control protocols require tranexamic acid.

variants.composition: Each 5 ml contains: Tranexamic Acid IP 500 mg in aqueous vehicle.
variants.usage: Often reviewed for obstetric emergency readiness, surgical bleeding protocols, and gynaecology care.

name: Oxytocin Injection IP
description: Synthetic oxytocin in PFS format for obstetric protocols including active management of the third stage of labour.
usageSummary: Used in labour rooms and obstetric care environments where cold-chain supported oxytocin is stocked for clinician-led maternal care protocols.

variants.composition: Each ml contains: Oxytocin IP 10 IU.
variants.usage: Typically reviewed by maternity hospitals and labour-room procurement teams for cold-chain obstetric inventory.
